# AI Registry / KI Register — Complete Platform Reference

> This file contains the full reference documentation for AI Registry / KI Register, a use-case-first AI governance platform for EU AI Act compliance. English platform: airegist.com — German/DACH platform: kiregister.com. Intended for large-context LLMs, AI agents, and automated systems that need complete platform knowledge in a single document.

---

## 1. Product Overview

**AI Registry** (English name) / **KI Register** (German name) is a B2B SaaS platform that helps organisations document, assess, and govern their AI systems in compliance with the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689).

The platform is built on a "use-case-first" approach: every AI system is registered as a concrete use case with structured attributes, rather than abstract policy documents. Governance, risk assessment, and evidence management are layered on top of this inventory.

**English domain:** https://airegist.com
**German / DACH domain:** https://kiregister.com
**Languages:** German (/de/) and English (/en/) — automatic browser language detection
**Data residency:** European Union (GDPR compliant)
**Legal controllers:** ZukunftBilden GmbH + BewusstseinBilden UG (haftungsbeschränkt), Austria — joint controllers per GDPR Art. 26

---

## 2. EU AI Act Context

The EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) entered into force on 2 August 2024. It applies to:
- **Providers** — organisations that develop or place AI systems on the market
- **Deployers** — organisations that use AI systems in a professional context

AI Registry is primarily built for **deployers** — the majority of organisations that use off-the-shelf AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Salesforce Einstein) and must document and govern their use.

Key compliance milestones:
- Prohibited AI systems: enforceable from February 2025
- GPAI model obligations: August 2025
- High-risk AI system obligations (Annex III): August 2026
- Full application including other high-risk systems: August 2027

---

## 3. Core Data Model

### Use Case (Einsatzfall)

The central entity. Fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `toolId` | string | Identifier of the AI tool/system in use |
| `purpose` | string | What the AI system is used for |
| `riskClass` | enum | MINIMAL, LIMITED, HIGH, PROHIBITED, UNASSESSED |
| `usageContexts` | array | INTERNAL_ONLY, EMPLOYEES, CUSTOMERS, PUBLIC, BUSINESS_PARTNERS, AUTOMATED_DECISION |
| `dataCategories` | array | NO_PERSONAL_DATA, PERSONAL_DATA, SPECIAL_PERSONAL, FINANCIAL_DATA, HEALTH_DATA, BIOMETRIC_DATA |
| `decisionInfluence` | enum | ASSISTANCE, PREPARATION, AUTOMATED |
| `status` | enum | UNREVIEWED, REVIEW_RECOMMENDED, REVIEWED, PROOF_READY |
| `oversightModel` | enum | HITL (human-in-the-loop), HOTL (human-on-the-loop), HUMAN_REVIEW, NO_HUMAN |
| `reviewCycle` | enum | annual, semiannual, quarterly, monthly, ad_hoc |
| `lifecycleStatus` | enum | active, paused, retired |
| `responsibleParty` | string | Who is responsible for this use case |
| `riskAssessment` | object | EU AI Act assessment results |
| `publicInfo` | object | Public-facing information for trust portal |
| `externalIntake` | object | If submitted via external intake form |

### Register

An organisation's complete collection of use cases. One register per organisation (workspace). Contains all use cases plus organisation-level governance settings.

### Organisation / Workspace

Maps to a company or organisational unit. Has:
- Members with roles (owner, member, external officer)
- Governance settings (RACI matrix, incident process, review standards, AI policy)
- Billing entitlement (Free / Pro / Enterprise)
- Access codes for external intake submissions

---

## 4. Risk Classification

### EU AI Act Four-Tier Model

**Prohibited (Unacceptable Risk)**
- Social scoring by public authorities
- Real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces (with exceptions)
- Subliminal manipulation causing harm
- Exploitation of vulnerabilities of specific groups
- Biometric categorisation inferring sensitive attributes
- Emotion recognition in workplace/education

**High-Risk (Annex III)**
Specific categories where significant documentation and human oversight are legally required:
1. Biometric identification and categorisation
2. Critical infrastructure management
3. Education and vocational training (student assessment, admissions)
4. Employment and workers management (CV screening, performance evaluation)
5. Essential private and public services (credit scoring, benefits eligibility)
6. Law enforcement (risk assessment of individuals, polygraphs)
7. Migration, asylum, border control
8. Administration of justice and democratic processes

**Limited Risk (Transparency Obligations)**
- Chatbots and conversational AI — must disclose AI identity to users
- Emotion recognition systems — must inform affected persons
- Deep fakes — must label synthetic content
- General-purpose AI with systemic risk

**Minimal Risk**
- AI-enabled video games
- Spam filters
- Most productivity AI (writing assistance, translation, summarisation)
- Recommendation systems in low-stakes contexts

---

## 5. Platform Features by Tier

### Free Register (All users)

**Use Case Registration**
- Add, edit and delete AI use cases with full metadata
- Risk classification (manually set or via assessment wizard)
- Status tracking (UNREVIEWED → REVIEW_RECOMMENDED → REVIEWED → PROOF_READY)
- Usage context tagging, data category tagging, decision influence classification

**EU AI Act Law Viewer**
- Full text of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689
- Recitals (1–180), Articles (1–113), Annexes
- Searchable, readable interface

**External Intake / Access Codes**
- Generate shareable access codes for team members or partners
- Recipients submit AI use cases via public form at /intake — no login needed
- Submissions appear in the register as traceable external entries

**Basic Risk Assessment**
- Guided assessment wizard per use case
- Maps to EU AI Act Annex III categories
- Outputs risk classification recommendation

### Governance Control Centre (Pro / Enterprise)

**Policy Generator (3 levels)**
- Level 1: Commitment Policy (1 page) — executive sign-off document
- Level 2: Framework Policy (3–5 pages) — operational guidelines
- Level 3: Enterprise Policy (8–10 pages) — comprehensive governance document
- AI writing assistant for defensive policy language
- Link policies to specific use cases

**Audit Export**
- Export complete governance dossier as PDF
- Structured data export (AIMS format)
- Per use case evidence packages
- Organisation-level audit report

**Trust Portal**
- Public-facing governance page per organisation
- Shows: which AI systems are in use, risk classifications, governance status
- Verifiable quality seal with certificate code
- Accessible without login — for auditors, partners, customers

**Portfolio Analytics**
- Risk/value matrix across all registered use cases
- Compliance exposure detection (missing assessments, missing oversight, prohibited systems)
- Portfolio-level governance health score
- Benchmarking insights

**ISO Alignment**
- ISO 42001 (AI management systems) — requirement mapping
- ISO 27001 — security control alignment
- Gap analysis per use case

**Review Workflows**
- Formal review documentation per use case
- Deadline assignment and tracking
- Overdue / due-soon / on-track status
- Review history audit trail

**Governance Settings (RACI + Standards)**
- RACI matrix: who is Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed per AI governance process
- Incident response process definition
- Review standards and cycles organisation-wide
- Competency and training requirements

**Supply Chain Assessment**
- Evaluate AI governance of vendors and suppliers
- Document supply chain AI risk
- Supplier invitation and verification workflow

### Enterprise (additional to Pro)

- Multi-organisation structure (subsidiaries, divisions)
- Advanced executive reporting dashboards
- Custom API integrations
- Dedicated onboarding and support

---

## 6. Industry-Specific Guidance

### Healthcare & Life Sciences

**EU AI Act risk level:** Most clinical AI is **High-Risk** (Annex III, Category 6 — critical medical devices overlap; Category 5(a) — essential private services including healthcare).

**Common use cases to register:**
- AI-assisted diagnostics (radiology, pathology)
- Patient deterioration prediction / early warning systems
- Clinical decision support tools
- Administrative AI (appointment scheduling, billing coding)
- Drug interaction checkers

**Key documentation requirements:**
- Risk assessment per system with justification for risk class
- Human oversight model: HITL required for clinical decisions affecting patient safety
- Data categories: HEALTH_DATA, SPECIAL_PERSONAL — triggers GDPR Art. 9 special category obligations
- Review cycle: minimum annual, recommended quarterly for active clinical systems

**Compliance exposure triggers in AI Registry:**
- Health data + AUTOMATED decision influence → flags as probable high-risk
- No human oversight documented on health-data system → compliance warning

---

### HR & Recruitment

**EU AI Act risk level:** **High-Risk** (Annex III, Category 4 — employment and workers management). This is one of the most scrutinised categories.

**Covered systems:**
- CV / resume screening tools
- Candidate ranking and shortlisting algorithms
- Interview analysis AI (video, tone, language)
- Performance evaluation systems
- Workforce planning and scheduling AI
- Employee monitoring tools

**Intersecting regulation:**
- GDPR Art. 22: Automated decision-making — employees and candidates have the right not to be subject to solely automated decisions with significant effects
- Works council / Betriebsrat consultation requirements in DACH
- Equal treatment obligations (AGG in Germany, GlBG in Austria)

**Required documentation in AI Registry:**
- Decision influence: must document whether AI is ASSISTANCE, PREPARATION, or AUTOMATED
- Oversight model: HITL or at minimum HOTL for hiring/firing decisions
- Data categories: PERSONAL_DATA minimum; SPECIAL_PERSONAL if processing photos, voice, or inferred characteristics
- Formal risk assessment required before deployment

---

### Finance & Banking

**EU AI Act risk level:** Mixed — **High-Risk** for credit scoring (Annex III, Category 5(b)), **Limited Risk** for many analytical tools.

**Common use cases:**
- Credit scoring and loan decisions
- Fraud detection (real-time transaction analysis)
- AML (anti-money laundering) transaction monitoring
- Algorithmic trading signals / robo-advisory
- Customer service chatbots
- KYC (know your customer) identity verification
- Insurance risk assessment

**Intersecting regulation:**
- MiFID II: algorithmic trading obligations
- EBA guidelines on internal governance including AI
- DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act): AI systems in ICT risk scope
- GDPR Art. 22: automated credit decisions — individuals have the right to human review

**Key documentation requirements:**
- Credit scoring systems: mandatory risk assessment + transparency documentation
- Chatbots: must disclose AI identity (Limited Risk transparency obligation)
- Fraud/AML: typically institutional internal use → INTERNAL_ONLY context + HITL oversight

---

### Education & Training

**EU AI Act risk level:** **High-Risk** (Annex III, Category 3) when used by educational institutions to assess students, determine access, or influence educational pathways.

**Covered systems:**
- Automated essay grading
- Student performance prediction / early intervention
- Admissions scoring tools
- Learning path personalisation
- Plagiarism and academic integrity detection
- Attendance and engagement monitoring

**Key considerations:**
- Applies to educational institutions (universities, schools, training providers)
- Consumer-facing tutoring apps generally minimal risk
- Student data: combination of PERSONAL_DATA + potentially SPECIAL_PERSONAL (health/disability accommodations)
- Parent/guardian notification requirements for minors

---

### Public Sector & Government

**EU AI Act risk level:** Highest scrutiny. Several categories are either **High-Risk** or **Prohibited**.

**High-risk categories for public sector:**
- Benefits eligibility determination (social welfare, unemployment)
- Public procurement evaluation
- Traffic management and infrastructure AI
- Emergency response prioritisation

**Near-prohibited / maximum scrutiny:**
- Social scoring by public bodies → **Prohibited**
- Predictive policing → **Prohibited** (risk assessment of individuals to predict criminal offences)
- Real-time biometric surveillance → **Prohibited** with narrow exceptions
- Border control / asylum AI → High-risk, Annex III Category 7

**Compliance requirements:**
- Fundamental rights impact assessment (FRIA) required for high-risk public sector AI
- Democratic oversight and public accountability documentation
- Transparency: citizens must be informed when AI is used in decisions affecting them

---

### Legal & Consulting

**EU AI Act risk level:** Typically **Limited Risk** or **Minimal Risk**. High-risk possible if AI is used in administration of justice (Annex III, Category 8).

**Common use cases:**
- Contract review and analysis AI
- Legal research assistants (case law retrieval, summarisation)
- Document automation
- Compliance monitoring tools
- Client-facing chatbots and intake tools
- Billing and matter management AI

**Key considerations:**
- Legal research/summarisation AI: Minimal Risk — no transparency obligation, but best practice to document
- If AI assists judges or tribunals in decision-making: High-Risk (Annex III, Category 8)
- Client-facing chatbots: Limited Risk — must disclose AI interaction
- Privilege and confidentiality: data category documentation critical (PERSONAL_DATA, sometimes SPECIAL_PERSONAL for client matters)

---

### Manufacturing & Operations

**EU AI Act risk level:** Generally **Minimal Risk** to **Limited Risk**, unless safety-critical.

**Common use cases:**
- Predictive maintenance (equipment failure prediction)
- Quality control vision AI (defect detection)
- Supply chain demand forecasting
- Energy optimisation
- Robotics process control
- Worker safety monitoring

**Safety-critical exceptions (High-Risk):**
- AI controlling safety components in machinery covered by EU Machinery Directive → High-Risk
- AI in critical infrastructure (energy grid, water supply) → High-Risk Annex III Category 2

**Key documentation:**
- Mostly INTERNAL_ONLY context + NO_PERSONAL_DATA → typically minimal risk
- If processing worker biometrics (badge access, safety monitoring) → BIOMETRIC_DATA → risk class review needed
- Supply chain AI: use the supply chain assessment feature to document vendor AI governance

---

## 7. API Reference

AI Registry provides a REST API for programmatic access to register data.

**Authentication:** Bearer token (Firebase Auth JWT or API key from Agent Kit)
**Base URL (EN):** https://airegist.com/api
**Base URL (DE/DACH):** https://kiregister.com/api

### Key Endpoints

`GET /api/workspaces` — List workspaces the authenticated user has access to

`GET /api/workspaces/{orgId}/registers` — Get registers for a workspace

`GET /api/workspaces/{orgId}/external-submissions` — List external intake submissions

`POST /api/workspaces/{orgId}/external-submissions` — Create an external submission

`GET /api/workspaces/{orgId}/members` — List workspace members

`GET /api/registers/{registerId}/external-submissions` — Get submissions for a specific register

`POST /api/supplier-invite` — Create a supplier governance assessment invitation

`GET /api/supplier-invite/list` — List supplier invitations

`POST /api/invites` — Create a workspace member invitation

`GET /api/capture-by-code?code={code}` — Validate an access code and get the target register info (public, no auth required)

`GET /api/certification` — Get certification status for the authenticated user

`POST /api/draft-assessment` — Generate a draft EU AI Act risk assessment using AI

`POST /api/policy/improve` — AI writing assistant to improve AI policy text

`GET /api/workspaces/{orgId}/audit-export` — Export full governance audit dossier

### Agent Kit

The Agent Kit provides structured access for AI agents:
- Configured per workspace in Settings → Agent Kit & API
- Returns governance summaries, risk exposure reports, use case metadata in agent-friendly format
- Useful for: Slack bots reporting compliance status, CI/CD gates on AI system changes, ERP integration for new tool onboarding

---

## 8. Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Deployer** | Organisation using an AI system in a professional context (vs. Provider who builds it) |
| **Provider** | Organisation that develops or places an AI system on the market |
| **Use Case / Einsatzfall** | A concrete AI application registered in the platform |
| **Risk Class** | EU AI Act tier: Minimal, Limited, High-Risk, or Prohibited |
| **HITL** | Human-in-the-loop — a human reviews/approves every AI output before it affects a person |
| **HOTL** | Human-on-the-loop — AI operates autonomously but human can intervene |
| **FRIA** | Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment — required for high-risk public sector AI |
| **Governance Control Centre** | AI Registry Pro/Enterprise tier with policy, audit, trust portal features |
| **Trust Portal** | Public governance evidence page, accessible without login |
| **Access Code** | Shareable code for external submission of use cases without login |
| **RACI** | Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed — governance assignment matrix |
| **Annex III** | The list of high-risk AI application categories in the EU AI Act |
| **GPAI** | General-Purpose AI (e.g. large language models) — subject to transparency and FRIA obligations |
| **Proof Ready** | Status indicating a use case has full documentation for regulatory evidence |

---

## 9. Legal and Data Protection

**Joint Controllers (GDPR Art. 26):**
- ZukunftBilden GmbH, Salzburg, Austria
- BewusstseinBilden UG (haftungsbeschränkt), Germany

**Data location:** European Union (Firebase/Google Cloud EU regions)

**Legal basis for processing (GDPR Art. 6):**
- Art. 6(1)(b): Contract performance — account and service delivery
- Art. 6(1)(c): Legal obligation — EU AI Act compliance documentation
- Art. 6(1)(f): Legitimate interest — security, fraud prevention, product improvement

**Data subject rights:** Access (Art. 15), Rectification (Art. 16), Erasure (Art. 17), Portability (Art. 20), Objection (Art. 21) — exercisable via privacy@airegist.com

**Governing law:** Austrian law. Jurisdiction: Landesgericht Salzburg.

**Consumer withdrawal (EU Consumer Rights Directive):** 14-day right of withdrawal for new subscriptions, starting from subscription date.

**EU ODR platform:** https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr

---
